name: Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – circular from Enguídanos to Cabeza Moya
name_original: Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – circular desde Enguídanos hasta el Cabeza Moya
region_intro: Jogging around Enguídanos offers diverse terrain within a rich natural landscape. The region is characterized by the Cabriel River and its tributaries, which have carved out numerous gorges and valleys. Extensive pine forests and surrounding hills provide varied environments for running, from riverside paths to more challenging ascents. This area is known for its abundant water features, including waterfalls and crystal-clear pools, enhancing the scenic appeal of its running routes. Best jogging routes around Enguídanos The most popular jogging route is Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – short loop from Enguídanos, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Cabriel River and surrounding landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – circular from Enguídanos to Cabeza Moya, a difficult 7.0 miles (11.3 km) path. This route provides panoramic vistas, including those of Cabeza Moya alongside the Cabriel River. Local runners also love the View of the River loop from Enguídanos, a 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through river valleys and forested areas, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Jogging around Enguídanos is defined by river valleys, dramatic gorges, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate riverside paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 15 runners have used komoot to explore Enguídanos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Enguídanos area?
A: The Enguídanos region offers a diverse network of trails for runners. This guide features 8 curated routes, ranging from moderate to difficult, showcasing the best of the area's natural beauty. Overall, the region has numerous well-marked trails, including both short-distance (PR routes) and long-distance (GR routes) paths.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Enguídanos?
A: While many routes in Enguídanos are rated moderate to difficult due to varied terrain and elevation changes, there are sections of trails that can be adapted for easier runs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40300865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Campillo de Altobuey&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4 km option. Generally, riverside paths offer less challenging terrain, though specific easy routes are not explicitly highlighted in this selection.

Q: Which running routes offer the best views or scenic points in Enguídanos?
A: Enguídanos is rich in stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014651906/sendero-de-los-cuatro-rios-circular-from-enguidanos-to-cabeza-moya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – circular from Enguídanos to Cabeza Moya&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views, including those of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Moya&lt;/a&gt; alongside the Cabriel River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the River loop from Enguídanos&lt;/a&gt; also leads through picturesque river valleys and forested areas. Additionally, the region boasts several natural viewpoints like Mirador de la Virgen and Mirador de la Cueva.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Enguídanos?
A: Yes, several excellent circular running routes are available. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014652903/sendero-de-los-cuatro-rios-short-loop-from-enguidanos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – short loop from Enguídanos&lt;/a&gt; (7.0 km) and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014651906/sendero-de-los-cuatro-rios-circular-from-enguidanos-to-cabeza-moya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – circular from Enguídanos to Cabeza Moya&lt;/a&gt; (11.3 km). These loops allow you to start and finish in the same location, making logistics easier.

Q: Are there any running trails that pass by waterfalls or significant water features?
A: Absolutely! Enguídanos is renowned for its abundant water features. The PR-CU 53 trail, also known as the "Sendero de las Chorreras," is a 13.5 km route that takes you through the remarkable aquatic environment of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt;, featuring waterfalls, crystal-clear pools, and uniquely sculpted rock formations. You'll also find numerous springs and other river features along many routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Enguídanos?
A: The terrain in Enguídanos is highly varied, offering a mix of experiences for runners. You can expect flat riverside paths, gentle ascents through extensive pine forests, and more challenging routes that navigate rugged gorges and valleys. This diversity provides both scenic beauty and varied workouts.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in the Enguídanos area?
A: For longer runs, consider sections of the GR-66 (Cabriel Valley Trail), which includes segments like Enguídanos-Mira (25 km) and Víllora-Enguídanos (16.5 km). Within this guide, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the River loop from Enguídanos&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 13.1 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014651906/sendero-de-los-cuatro-rios-circular-from-enguidanos-to-cabeza-moya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – circular from Enguídanos to Cabeza Moya&lt;/a&gt; is 11.3 km, both offering significant distances.

Q: What other attractions can I explore near the running routes in Enguídanos?
A: Beyond the trails, Enguídanos offers several points of interest. You can visit the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6098023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enguídanos&lt;/a&gt; itself, explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the natural beauty of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3585363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertecillo de Yemeda&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic mountain pass experience.

Q: Is Enguídanos suitable for trail running, given its landscape?
A: Yes, Enguídanos is an excellent destination for trail running. Its rugged and winding landscapes, characterized by gorges like Hoz del Agua and Hoz Cerrada, along with extensive pine forests and varied elevation, provide an ideal environment for trail runners seeking diverse challenges and stunning natural backdrops.

Q: Are there any specific routes that highlight the Cabriel River?
A: The Cabriel River is central to the Enguídanos landscape. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014652903/sendero-de-los-cuatro-rios-short-loop-from-enguidanos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – short loop from Enguídanos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014651906/sendero-de-los-cuatro-rios-circular-from-enguidanos-to-cabeza-moya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos – circular from Enguídanos to Cabeza Moya&lt;/a&gt; offer direct views and proximity to the Cabriel River, allowing runners to experience its clean waters and dramatic valley.

Q: What makes the Enguídanos region unique for outdoor activities like jogging?
A: Enguídanos stands out due to its exceptional natural landscapes, particularly its rich water features. With five rivers, including the pristine Cabriel, numerous waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras&lt;/a&gt;, and dramatic gorges, the area offers a visually stimulating and invigorating experience. The diverse terrain, from riverside paths to mountain foothills, ensures a variety of running environments.
tips: One of the many panoramic points offered by the Cuatro Ríos trail, in this case with beautiful views of Cabeza Moya flanked by the Cabriel River on its way to the Contreras Reservoir.
